input.initialPrompt: Analyze code coverage and risk hotspots for this project. I haven't run any coverage yet.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to analyze code coverage and risk hotspots for this .NET project. They haven't run any coverage yet, so I need to:

1. First explore the project structure to understand what we're working with
2. Set up and run code coverage
3. Analyze the results

Let me start by exploring the project structure.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests with code coverage collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with code coverage collection

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture of the project. Let me run the tests with code coverage collection and then analyze the results. The project already has `coverlet.collector` as a dependency, so I can use that.

Let me run the tests with coverage in Cobertura format so I can parse the results.

arguments.description: Run tests with code coverage collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-444b64afd67a4a6fa34529c6dea1f2ee && dotnet test --collect:\"XPlat Code Coverage\" --results-directory ./coverage-results -- DataCollectionRunSettings.DataCollectors.DataCollector.Configuration.Format=cobertura 2>&1", "description": "Run tests with code coverage collection", "initial_wait": 60}
